purpose: Define the boundary between v1 closed-loop assets and the v2 public SDK/runtime surface.

legacy_dirs.policy: Do not restore package exports, SDK namespaces, runner imports, or public workflows that execute closed-loop modules. Archive content is evidence only unless an operator explicitly restores it for migration.

allowed_references.reason: One-time migration utility can read legacy PRD JSON if an operator restores archived evidence for migration.
Memory audit classifies legacy JSONL paths as read-only evidence but does not import or execute legacy modules.
Learning migration reads legacy closed-loop JSONL knowledge as data and writes normalized state/learning.jsonl records without importing or executing legacy modules.
Package smoke forbids closed-loop files from entering the public tarball; it must not import or execute closed-loop modules.
Release readiness checks package files allowlist entries for legacy closed-loop leakage; it must not import or execute closed-loop modules.

prohibited: package.json exports or bin targets under closed-loop/
src/**/*.ts imports, dynamic imports, or child-process execution of closed-loop modules
new root entrypoints that wrap closed-loop modules as public SDK features
